zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Yelverton於大約1425年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可時，她的父親Sir William Yelverton of Rougham27歲，母親Joan Le Gross36歲。她和Sir Thomas Tyndale至少有2個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1513年9月11日在英國英格蘭諾福爾可海黑瑟特享年89歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可諾維其。

nameMeanings.name: Yelverton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon): habitational name from either of two places called Yelverton, in Norfolk or Devon. Medieval examples of the surname are from the Norfolk place, but the modern surname in Britain is associated mainly with Devon, where the earliest record of the surname is found in 1761, and is probably a substitution for Elford , an earlier form of the Devon placename. The Devon place, a hamlet in Buckland Monachorum, is not officially on record as Yelverton until the 19th century, when the Great Western Railway so named the station it built there in 1859. It is a dialect pronunciation of Elverton, which is first recorded in 1765 as an extended form of its medieval name Elford + -ton ‘town’. The Norfolk placename is recorded as Ailuertuna in 1086, and probably derives from the Old English personal name Æthelfrith or Geldfrith + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Elford
meanings: English: habitational name from Yelverton in Buckland Monachorum (Devon), recorded as Elleford in 1291 (see Yelverton ), named from the Old English personal name Ella or elle, ellen ‘elder tree’ + ford ‘ford’. The name may also derive from any of the places called Elford in Staffordshire and Northumberland, both probably from the Old English personal name Ella + ford.

variants.name: Yelvington
meanings: Probably an altered form of English   Yelverton  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Yelverton was born about 1425, in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir William Yelverton of Rougham, was 27 and her mother, Joan Le Gross, was 36. She had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with Sir Thomas Tyndale. She died on 11 September 1513, in Hethersett, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 89, and was buried in Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles avec Sir Thomas Tyndale. Elle est décédée le 11 septembre 1513, à Hethersett, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 89 ans, et a été enterrée à Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Yelverton(이)가 대략 1425년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Yelverton of Rougham(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Le Gross(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 Sir Thomas Tyndale(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1513년 9월 11일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 헤더세트에서 89세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 노위치에 묻혔다.
